top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer is anchored on the four-fold compound (vihāya kāmān / niḥspṛha / nirmama / nirahaṅkāra) preserved verbatim with each component glossed in the Sanskrit. Shankara's 'without the conceit of being learned' gloss is attributed by name. The 'this is MY code' / 'I am the engineer who shipped this' framing is anchored in the privative compounds nir-mama and nir-ahaṅkāra. No other Gita verse runs this exact four-fold compound with this structural force at the chapter's penultimate position.

BG 12.13 (adveṣṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca / nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣamī) shares the nirmama-nirahaṅkāra compound. But 12.13 is in the bhakti context with the additional adveṣṭā / maitra / karuṇa specifications absent in 2.71. The 'this is MY code' anchor on the artifact-identity uncoupling is closer to 2.71's penultimate-state framing than to 12.13's universal-disposition framing.

BG 18.53 (ahaṅkāraṁ balaṁ darpaṁ kāmaṁ krodhaṁ parigraham / vimucya nirmamaḥ śānto brahma-bhūyāya kalpate) shares the nirmama compound and śānti-attaining theme. But 18.53 is in the chapter's renunciation-doctrine context, with the additional ahaṅkāra-bala-darpa-kāma-krodha-parigraha list. Distant possibility ranked third for completeness.
